reasoning: The target temperature threshold is the value on 2025-10-26, which is 7.1375 °C. Weather data shows an acute cold snap over the preceding几天, dropping from ~13.8 °C down to 7.1 °C. Expecting a mean reversion, the short-term forecast naturally implies temperatures rebounding toward seasonal averages (around 8.5-9 °C for early Nov). Thus, for 2025-11-02, the probability of exceeding 7.1 °C is solidly above 50% (estimated at 0.65). For later dates (late November and late January), the climatological base rate drops as those months become progressively colder. A normal base rate for >7.1 °C in late Nov is around 35%, and for late Jan around 25%. However, strictly following the instruction to mathematically increase uncertainty with forecast horizon ("probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5"), I am actively bleeding these natural base rates towards 0.5 over longer horizons. Therefore, the distance to 0.5 decreases monotonically from 0.15 at 7 days, to 0.08 at 30 days, to 0.03 at 90 days.
